back_translation: Samson went to Gaza, saw a prostitute there, and went in to her.

The Gazites were told, 'Samson has come here.' They surrounded the place and lay in wait for him all night at the gate of the city. They were quiet all night, saying, 'At the light of morning, we will kill him.'

Samson lay until midnight, then arose at midnight, took hold of the doors of the city gate and the two posts, pulled them up along with the bolt, placed them on his shoulders, and carried them to the top of the hill that faces Hebron.
After this, he loved a woman in the Valley of Sorek, whose name was Delilah.

The lords of the Philistines came up to her and said, 'Entice him, and see in what his great strength lies, and how we can overpower him so that we may bind him to subdue him. We will each give you eleven hundred pieces of silver.'
Delilah said to Samson, 'Please tell me in what your great strength lies and with what you can be bound to subdue you.'
Samson said to her, 'If they bind me with seven fresh bowstrings that have not been dried, then I will become weak and be like any other man.'
The lords of the Philistines brought up to her seven fresh bowstrings that had not been dried, and she bound him with them.

While the ambushers were sitting in her chamber, she said to him, 'Philistines are upon you, Samson!' But he broke the bowstrings as a strand of flax breaks when it touches fire. So the secret of his strength was not known.
Delilah said to Samson, 'Behold, you have mocked me and told me lies. Now please tell me with what you can be bound.'
He said to her, 'If they really bind me with new ropes with which no work has been done, then I will become weak and be like any other man.'
Delilah took new ropes, bound him with them, and said to him, 'Philistines are upon you, Samson!' (The ambushers were sitting in the chamber.) But he broke them from his arms like thread.
Delilah said to Samson, 'Until now you have mocked me and told me lies. Tell me with what you can be bound.' He said to her, 'If you weave the seven locks of my head with the web of the loom.'
She fastened it with the pin and said to him, 'Philistines are upon you, Samson!' He awoke from his sleep and pulled out the pin of the loom and the web.
She said to him, 'How can you say, I love you, when your heart is not with me? Three times now you have mocked me and not told me in what your great strength lies.'
When she pressed him with her words daily and urged him, his soul was shortened to die.

He told her all his heart and said to her, 'A razor has never come upon my head, for I have been a Nazirite to God from my mother's womb. If I am shaved, then my strength will leave me, and I will become weak and be like any other man.'

Delilah saw that he had told her all his heart, and she sent and called for the lords of the Philistines, saying, 'Come up this time, for he has told me all his heart.' The lords of the Philistines came up to her and brought the silver in their hands.
She lulled him to sleep on her knees and called a man, and she shaved the seven locks of his head. She began to subdue him, and his strength left from upon him.
She said, 'Philistines are upon you, Samson!' He awoke from his sleep and said, 'I will go out as time after time and shake myself free.' But he did not know that the LORD had departed from him.
The Philistines seized him, gouged out his eyes, brought him down to Gaza, bound him with bronze shackles, and he became a grinder in the prison house.
But the hair of his head began to grow back after it had been shaved.
The lords of the Philistines gathered to offer a great sacrifice to Dagon their god and to rejoice. They said, 'Our god has given into our hand Samson our enemy.'
The people saw him and praised their god, for they said, 'Our god has given into our hand our enemy, the destroyer of our land, the one who has multiplied our slain.'
When their hearts were merry, they said, 'Call for Samson, that he may play for us.' They called for Samson from the prison house, and he played before them. They made him stand between the pillars.
Samson said to the young man who held his hand, 'Lead me, and let me feel the pillars on which the house stands, that I may lean on them.'
The house was full of men and women. All the lords of the Philistines were there, and on the roof about three thousand men and women were watching while Samson played.
Samson called to the LORD and said, 'O Lord GOD, please remember me. Please strengthen me only this once, O God, that I may avenge with one vengeance from the Philistines for my two eyes.'
Samson took hold of the two central pillars on which the house stood, and he leaned upon them — one with his right hand and one with his left.

Samson said, 'Let my soul die with the Philistines.' He bowed himself with strength, and the house fell on the lords and on all the people who were in it. The dead whom he killed in his death were more than those whom he had killed in his life.
His brothers and all the household of his father came down, took him, came up, and buried him between Zorah and Eshtaol in the tomb of Manoah his father. He had judged Israel twenty years.
